evals.prompt: Update my research on the enterprise observability market. I want to refresh all dimensions with the latest data.
evals.expected_output: The skill locates the active research session via state.json, loads prior state and elicitation, delegates to research-orchestrator in update mode with all dimensions, performs delta detection, and produces an updated state.json with a new lineage entry and delta report

deterministic_checks.description: Skill searches for or reads state.json to locate the active research session
Skill delegates to the research-orchestrator agent
Orchestrator is spawned in update mode
Delta detection is enabled by default

expectations: The skill reads state.json to load prior findings, elicitation, lineage, and dimensions
The research-orchestrator is spawned with MODE: update and receives the prior elicitation
All dimensions from the prior research are included in the update delegation
Delta detection is enabled by default since --no-delta was not specified
The output references a summary of changes: new, updated, confirmed, or removed findings

evals.expected_output: The skill uses the specified topic slug, updates only the market-size and competitive-landscape dimensions, and passes the since date to the orchestrator for date-filtered queries

deterministic_checks.description: Skill resolves to the enterprise-observability topic slug
market-size dimension is included in the update
competitive-landscape dimension is included in the update
The since date is passed to the orchestrator

expectations: Only market-size and competitive-landscape dimensions are updated, not all dimensions
The SINCE_DATE parameter is passed to the orchestrator so analysts prioritize data from 2026-01-01 onward
The skill does NOT re-run elicitation -- it reuses the prior elicitation from state.json

evals.expected_output: The skill performs a full refresh of existing research with delta detection disabled, meaning findings are replaced wholesale without NEW/UPDATED/CONFIRMED/POTENTIALLY_REMOVED classification

deterministic_checks.description: Skill locates and reads state.json
Delta detection is explicitly disabled
Skill still delegates to the research-orchestrator

expectations: Delta detection is disabled because --no-delta was specified
The orchestrator does NOT classify findings as NEW/UPDATED/CONFIRMED/POTENTIALLY_REMOVED
Findings are replaced wholesale rather than reconciled against prior findings
The rest of the update pipeline (orchestrator delegation, dimension analysts, merge) still executes normally

evals.expected_output: The skill maps the --area argument to the corresponding dimension and updates only that single dimension

deterministic_checks.description: The regulatory area is identified and mapped to a dimension
Skill loads prior state from state.json
Skill delegates to the research-orchestrator

expectations: The --area regulatory argument is mapped to a specific dimension from the prior elicitation
Only the resolved dimension is passed to the orchestrator, not all dimensions
Delta detection is enabled by default for the single-dimension update

evals.expected_output: When no state.json exists, the skill informs the user that no active research session was found and suggests using /sigint:start

deterministic_checks.description: Skill searches for state.json files
Skill reports that no active research session was found
Skill suggests using /sigint:start to begin a new session

expectations: The skill searches for state.json files using Glob
When no state.json is found, the skill stops and does NOT attempt to create a new session
The error message suggests using /sigint:start to begin research

evals.prompt: Update my research. I have sessions for both enterprise-observability and ai-coding-assistants.
evals.expected_output: When multiple sessions exist and no --topic is specified, the skill lists all sessions and asks the user to specify which one to update

deterministic_checks.description: Skill searches for state.json files
Skill asks the user to choose which session to update

expectations: The skill finds multiple state.json files across different topic directories
The skill lists all available sessions for the user to choose from
The skill does NOT arbitrarily pick one session -- it asks the user to specify

evals.expected_output: The skill performs an update on a single dimension, delegates to the orchestrator, receives delta detection results classifying findings as NEW/UPDATED/CONFIRMED/POTENTIALLY_REMOVED, and reconciles by replacing updated findings rather than appending

deterministic_checks.description: Skill uses the specified topic slug
Only market-size dimension is updated
Delta detection classifies findings using the defined categories
Findings are reconciled (replaced, not appended blindly)

expectations: Delta detection classifies findings using at least some of: NEW, UPDATED, CONFIRMED, POTENTIALLY_REMOVED, TREND_REVERSAL
Reconciliation replaces updated findings rather than blindly appending new ones alongside old
The updated state.json includes a new lineage entry recording this update

evals.expected_output: The skill combines --since date filtering with specific dimensions, passing SINCE_DATE to the orchestrator so analysts use date-filtered web searches. A delta report is generated showing changes since the prior update.

deterministic_checks.description: The since date is propagated to the orchestrator
competitive-landscape dimension is included
customer-research dimension is included
Delta detection is referenced in the output
Prior elicitation is loaded and passed to the orchestrator

expectations: The SINCE_DATE is included in analyst spawn prompts so they prioritize recent data via date-filtered searches
Only competitive-landscape and customer-research dimensions are updated
Prior elicitation from state.json is reused without re-running the elicitation phase
A delta report summarizing changes is generated or referenced in the output

evals.prompt: /sigint:update --topic enterprise-observability --dimensions competitive-landscape. The prior findings include a finding about 'Datadog acquires vendor X' that now has a substantially rewritten summary reflecting a new development: the acquisition closed and integration was announced.
evals.expected_output: The UPDATED finding for the Datadog acquisition includes a delta_detail object with change_category=substantive and newsworthiness=high, since the summary reflects a genuinely new factual development

deterministic_checks.description: UPDATED finding includes a delta_detail object
delta_detail includes a change_category field
The change is classified as substantive
delta_detail includes a newsworthiness signal

expectations: UPDATED findings carry a delta_detail object with changed_fields, change_category, and newsworthiness
A substantive summary rewrite reflecting a new factual development is classified as change_category=substantive
Substantive updates with new factual content receive newsworthiness=high
The newsworthiness_basis field explains why this is high newsworthiness

evals.prompt: /sigint:update --topic enterprise-observability --dimensions financial. A prior finding about 'Q1 earnings deadline is in 3 weeks' now says 'Q1 earnings deadline is in 9 days' with no other changes to the summary content.
evals.expected_output: The UPDATED finding is classified with change_category=temporal and newsworthiness=low, since only a time-relative reference changed

deterministic_checks.description: The change is classified as temporal
Temporal-only changes receive low newsworthiness

expectations: A finding whose summary changed only to update a time-relative reference (countdown, date arithmetic) is classified as temporal
Temporal-only changes receive newsworthiness=low because they are not substantively new information
The summary_diff describes the temporal shift (e.g., '3 weeks to 9 days')

evals.prompt: /sigint:update --topic enterprise-observability --dimensions market-size. A prior finding had confidence=medium and the new version has confidence=high due to a new authoritative source.
evals.expected_output: The UPDATED finding's delta_detail includes a confidence_change object recording the previous and current confidence values

deterministic_checks.description: delta_detail records the confidence change
confidence_change includes the previous value
confidence_change includes the current value

expectations: The confidence_change object records {previous: 'medium', current: 'high'} or equivalent
When confidence is the primary change, change_category is confidence_shift
A medium-to-high confidence shift receives newsworthiness=medium

evals.prompt: /sigint:update --topic enterprise-observability. The update produces 3 NEW findings, 2 UPDATED findings (1 substantive with high newsworthiness, 1 temporal with low newsworthiness), and 1 TREND_REVERSAL.
evals.expected_output: The lineage entry's delta_from_previous includes newsworthy_count=5 (3 new + 1 high-newsworthiness update + 1 trend reversal) and an update_breakdown showing substantive=1 and temporal=1

deterministic_checks.description: Lineage entry includes newsworthy_count
Lineage entry includes update_breakdown by change category

expectations: The delta_from_previous object is structured with counts for each classification type
newsworthy_count sums new_count + high-newsworthiness UPDATED count + trend_reversal_count
update_breakdown records the count for each change_category (substantive, temporal, etc.)
The structured delta_from_previous replaces the prior unstructured string format

evals.prompt: /sigint:update --topic enterprise-observability --dimensions competitive-landscape,market-size. Ensure the delta report includes a Newsworthy Changes section.
evals.expected_output: The generated delta report markdown includes a 'Newsworthy Changes' section consolidating all NEW findings, high-newsworthiness UPDATED findings, and TREND_REVERSAL findings

deterministic_checks.description: Delta report includes the Newsworthy Changes section
A delta report file is generated

expectations: The delta report markdown has a Newsworthy Changes section near the top of the report
The Newsworthy Changes section consolidates all items worth reporting for downstream consumers
The Updated Findings section is sub-categorized by change type (Substantive, Confidence, Temporal, Metadata)

evals.prompt: /sigint:update --topic enterprise-observability. The session has 600+ findings across 6 dimensions. Ensure the merge does not use --argjson for findings or sources arrays.
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator writes findings and sources to temporary files and uses --slurpfile to pass them to jq, avoiding shell ARG_MAX limits that would crash with 500+ findings passed as --argjson command-line arguments

deterministic_checks.description: Merge command uses --slurpfile to pass findings/sources arrays
Findings are written to a temp file before jq merge
Temp files are cleaned up after the merge

expectations: The merge jq command uses --slurpfile for findings and sources arrays, not --argjson
Temporary files are created (e.g., /tmp/sigint_findings_$$.json) to hold large arrays before passing to jq
Temp files are removed after the merge completes
The same --slurpfile pattern applies to both full-mode and update-mode merges

evals.prompt: /sigint:update --topic enterprise-observability. This is the second update run. The findings_hashes.json file from the prior run exists and shows that 4 of 6 dimensions have unchanged file hashes. Only 2 dimensions have new findings.
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator detects that 4 dimensions are unchanged via SHA-256 hash comparison, skips tag normalization and delta detection for those dimensions, bulk-confirms their findings, and reports the skip count. After merge, findings_hashes.json is updated with current hashes for all dimensions.

deterministic_checks.description: Orchestrator loads or writes findings_hashes.json
SHA-256 hashes are computed for dimension findings files
Orchestrator reports skipped dimensions due to unchanged hashes
Skipped dimensions are bulk-classified as CONFIRMED without re-processing

expectations: The planned-update display (when no session exists) mentions incremental merge and SHA-256 hash verification
Dimensions whose findings_*.json hash matches the stored hash are marked SKIPPED and excluded from tag normalization
Skipped dimensions' findings are bulk-confirmed (last_confirmed updated) without running title-similarity matching
After merge, findings_hashes.json is rewritten with current hashes covering all dimensions (both changed and skipped)
The progress file reports: 'Dimensions skipped: [list]' or equivalent skip count

evals.prompt: /sigint:update --topic enterprise-observability. The update processes 8 dimensions: 5 changed, 3 unchanged (skipped by hash). The generated delta report should reflect the partition.
evals.expected_output: The delta report Summary section includes 'Dimensions processed: 5 changed, 3 skipped (unchanged)' and the Confirmed findings line shows the skip-confirmed count separately from title-match confirmed findings

deterministic_checks.description: Delta report summary line reports changed and skipped dimension counts
Confirmed count distinguishes skip-confirmed from title-match confirmed
Orchestrator tracks and reports changed vs skipped dimension sets

expectations: The delta report Summary section includes a 'Dimensions processed: N changed, N skipped (unchanged)' line
The Confirmed findings count breaks out 'N via dimension-level skip' and 'N via title match' separately
Delta detection (title-similarity matching) only runs against CHANGED_DIMENSIONS, not against all 8 dimensions
Skipped dimensions do not appear in the New/Updated/Potentially Removed sections of the delta report
